chunk_text: To qualify for Medicare home health services, a patient must be confined to the home (homebound), require intermittent skilled nursing care or physical/speech therapy, be under the care of a physician, and receive services under a plan of care established and periodically reviewed by a physician. Homebound status means leaving home requires considerable and taxing effort.

A patient is considered homebound if they have a condition due to illness or injury that restricts their ability to leave home except with the aid of supportive devices, special transportation, or the assistance of another person. Leaving home must require a considerable and taxing effort. Absences from home for medical treatment or adult day care do not disqualify the patient.

section_title: Covered Services Under a Home Health Plan of Care
chunk_text: Medicare covers the following home health services when ordered by a physician: skilled nursing care on a part-time or intermittent basis, physical therapy, speech-language pathology services, occupational therapy (when furnished alongside another qualifying service), medical social services, and home health aide services. All services must be medically necessary.

Medicare Part A covers inpatient hospital services when the beneficiary is formally admitted as an inpatient on the order of a physician. Coverage includes a semi-private room, meals, general nursing, drugs as part of inpatient treatment, and other hospital services and supplies. The benefit period begins on the first day of hospitalization.

section_title: Acupuncture for Chronic Low Back Pain
chunk_text: Medicare covers acupuncture for chronic low back pain (cLBP) for up to 12 visits in 90 days. An additional 8 sessions are covered for patients demonstrating improvement. Coverage requires that the cLBP is defined as lasting 12 weeks or longer, is not related to surgery, and is not accompanied by a fever or significant neurological deficit attributable to the back pain.

Medicare covers cardiac rehabilitation programs for beneficiaries who have experienced an acute myocardial infarction within the preceding 12 months, coronary artery bypass surgery, stable angina pectoris, heart valve repair or replacement, or a heart or heart-lung transplant. Programs include physician-prescribed exercise, cardiac risk factor modification, and psychosocial assessment.

Effective January 1 2021, CMS revised E/M coding guidelines. Physicians may select the E/M visit level based on either medical decision making (MDM) or total time on the date of the encounter. The history and physical examination are no longer required elements for selecting the level of office or outpatient E/M service. Proper documentation must support the selected code level.

Medicare claims must be filed no later than 12 months from the date of service. Claims submitted after the timely filing deadline will be denied. Exceptions to the timely filing limit may be granted when the delay was caused by administrative error by CMS or the Medicare contractor, when the beneficiary was covered by a primary payer, or in cases involving retroactive Medicare entitlement.

chapter_title: Covered Medical and Other Health Services
chunk_text: Medicare Part B covers durable medical equipment (DME) when prescribed by a physician for use in the patient's home. DME must be durable (able to withstand repeated use), used for a medical purpose, not useful to a person in the absence of illness or injury, and appropriate for home use. Covered items include wheelchairs, hospital beds, oxygen equipment, and CPAP devices.

chapter_title: Hospital Services Covered Under Part B
chunk_text: Medicare Part B covers outpatient hospital services that are medically necessary and ordered by a physician. Covered services include diagnostic tests, radiology services, physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech-language pathology, emergency department visits, observation services, and oncology treatments such as chemotherapy. The beneficiary is responsible for the Part B deductible and 20% coinsurance.
